ChangeRequest.name: [FINREP] F 44.04 Disclosures of Staff expenses
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
# Overview 2021_5793 Disclosures of Staff expenses Management body in its supervisory function in FINREP Template 44.4
In its Q&A, the EBA explicitly requires FINREP disclosure of remuneration/meeting fees under personnel expenses. I assume this is due to the comparability between the individual EU countries, as in many countries there is no separation between the management board and the supervisory board/board of directors, but only one board?? In principle, FINREP requires that this reporting system reflects the accounting. In this case, however, the EBA explicitly requires a certain disclosure, which in my view should be followed. This results in a breakthrough in the uniformity of accounting and FINREP disclosure.
Is my interpretation correct, that in the interests of uniformity, all reporting scopes for FinRep, shall comply with the EBA Q&A regardless of the scope of reporting. Meaning, also the ones not reporting F 44.4 need to separate these fees? How will that be reflected – you will only notice such cases, if you analyze FullFinRep reporting scope.
### Contact person @Arjan.Bos @andreamerz
### Related issues #482
